text:
Admission Date:  2019-11-16              Discharge Date:   1996-11-14

Date of Birth:  1979-05-06             Sex:   F

Service: MEDICINE

Allergies:
Patient recorded as having No Known Allergies to Drugs

Attending:Keith
Chief Complaint:
Post operative cardiac monitoring s/p pericardial effusion
drainage.

Major Surgical or Invasive Procedure:
Pericardial tap and drain placement

History of Present Illness:
Ms. Jackson is a 49 y.o woman with a h/o of smoking,
hypercholesterolemia, cardiomegaly, and emphysema with
pericardial effusion. On 10-01, the pt had an episode of RUQ
and epigastric pain. She was admitted at St. Mark's Medical Center and found to
have a pericardial effussion and right atrial mass. Since that
time she has undergone consultation with Dr. Melanie [**Last Name (Prefixes) **]
who recommended surgery and has a tentative surgical date of
2018-11-06. She is also being followed by an oncologist, Dr.
White under who's recommendation is that she has had a
mammogram, a PET scan and cytology of her pericardial fluid.
.
Ms. Jackson was admitted today for pericardial tap and Rt heart
cath. There are no perioperative complications. Rt heart cath
showed RA pr 10, PA pr 31/14/22, wedge 12, CO 3.9, CI 2.8, RV pr
31/7/11. Drained 360 cc straw colored fluid. She was transferred
to CCU for post op management.

ROS
Patient reports postop pleuritic pain near drain insertion site.

Denies headache, SOB, abd pain, hematuria, dysuria, N/V/D/C.


Past Medical History:
hypercholesterolemia
Right atrial mass/pericardial effusion
GERD, esophagitis via EGD, s/p dilation
Hiatal hernia
Removal of vocal cord polyp 1989-11-29 (St. Mark's Medical Center)
Prior heavy ETOH, Drinks 2-3 beers per day several times a week

Removal of a cyst on the left ovary
Ectopic pregnancy with removal of part of one tube
D&C
T&A
Emphysema
Migraine headaches


Social History:
Patient is single and lives with her sister
David Mccarthy Beth Carter. She has two children, ages 28 and 30. Her son
Michael Frazier Michael Frazier will accompany her to the hospital. His cell
phone number is 297-656-4707. She works as a data entry
supervisor. Patient has smoked up to 2.5 packs a day x 39 years.
Had smoked a pack a day over the past year or two. Quit briefly
but restarted smoking in 09.


Family History:
Father had a CABG at age 56 with subsequent MI's.
He passed away in his late 70's


Physical Exam:
vitals: bp 93/59 HR 68 RR 17 SpO2 96% on RA
Gen: Awake & alert, NAD, resting comfortably, reports post-op
pain with inspiration.
HEENT: EOMI, PERRLA, 2mm pupils, no rhinorrhea or lacrimation,
OP clear
Neck:soft & supple, no masses
Heart: distant heart sounds, RRR, S1 & S2 audible, no m/r/g
Lungs: CTAB, no wheezes, rales or rhonchi heard
Abdomen: soft, NT, ND, pericardial drain site C/D/I, no fluid
collection
Extremities: warm & well perfused, periph pulses intact (2+)


Pertinent Results:

Glucose: 100
LD(LDH): 185

PERICARDIAL FLUID
Other Body Fluid Hematology:
WBC: 377

Initial echo:
Large pericardial effusion with tamponade physiology. Right
atrial
mass. Preserved biventricular global and regional biventricular
systolic
function.

Follow up echo:
Overall left ventricular systolic function is normal (LVEF>55%).
There is a
trivial/physiologic pericardial effusion. There are no
echocardiographic signs
of tamponade.

Compared with the prior study (images reviewed) of 2019-11-16
post
pericardiocentesis, there is minimal change.


Brief Hospital Course:
pericardial effusion drainage: Pt had pericardial drainage with
pigtail catheter placement. over 400 cc pericardial fluid
drained total. Repeat echo showed improvement. The catheter was
removed and the patient was discharged home when she was feeling
better, with only slight pleuritic chest pain. EKG showed slight
evidence of pericarditis and she was put on standing ibuprofen.
She will follow up with cardiac surgery for myxoma removal in 2
days on 2018-11-06.


Medications on Admission:
Omeprazole 20mg twice a day
Lorazapam 0.5mg daily at bedtime
Advair diskus 100/50 one puff twice a day
Albuterol inhaler two puffs four times a day prn


Discharge Medications:
1. Lorazepam 0.5 mg Tablet Sig: One (1) Tablet PO HS (at
bedtime) as needed.
2. Fluticasone-Salmeterol 100-50 mcg/Dose Disk with Device Sig:
One (1) Disk with Device Inhalation St. Mark's Medical Center (2 times a day).
3. Albuterol 90 mcg/Actuation Aerosol Sig: 1-2 Puffs Inhalation
Q6H (every 6 hours).
4. Ibuprofen 600 mg Tablet Sig: One (1) Tablet PO Q6H (every 6
hours).
Disp:*120 Tablet(s)* Refills:*0*
5. Omeprazole 20 mg Capsule, Delayed Release(E.C.) Sig: One (1)
Capsule, Delayed Release(E.C.) PO once a day.
Disp:*30 Capsule, Delayed Release(E.C.)(s)* Refills:*2*


Discharge Disposition:
Home With Service

Facility:
[**Company 1519**]

Discharge Diagnosis:
1)Pericardial effusion s/p tap and drain placement
2)Right atrial mass

Discharge Condition:
Stable

Discharge Instructions:
1)Please continue with your home medications.

2)Please return for surgery on Monday 2018-11-06 as you have been
instructed.

3)If you experience any chest pain, SOB, lightheadedness,
dizziness or any concerning symptoms, please return to the
Emergency department.

Followup Instructions:
You will be contactMichael Frazier by Dr. [**Last Name (Prefixes) 68741**] or Dr. White
regarding further tests and appointments, likley surgery on
Monday 2018-11-06.
